zh.lifeSummary: 當Fernando GARCÍA DE HITA, señor de Hita, Uceda, Guadalajara y Medinacelli於1065年出生在西班牙坎塔布里亞吉洛利格 德 利艾巴納時，他的父親Conde Caballero Garcia Ordoñez de Aza Y de Nájera15歲，母親Urraca Garcés de Navarra31歲。他於1090年與Trigidia Fernández de Aza在西班牙結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1135年享年71歲。

nameMeanings.name: GARCÍA
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jesus, Carlos, Luis, Francisco, Pedro, Miguel, Jorge, Mario, Raul, Roberto, Ramon, Rafael.  Portuguese Wenceslao, Joao, Paulo, Catarina, Albeiro, Ligia, Godofredo, Lidio, Wenseslao, Adao, Afonso.

Spanish (García) and Portuguese: from a medieval personal name of uncertain origin. It is normally found in medieval records in the Latin form Garsea, and may well be of pre-Roman origin, perhaps akin to Basque (h)artz ‘bear’. This is the most common surname in Spain and also one of the most common surnames elsewhere in Spanish-speaking world. In the US, where it is also found among Native Americans (mainly Pueblos in NM), it is the sixth most frequent surname. Compare De Garcia .

variants.name: Grassia
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Amedeo, Maurizio, Pasquale, Rocco, Salvatore.
Italian: from a metathesized form of the personal nameGarsia, from Spanish García (see Garcia ).

variants.name: Arcia
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Alberto, Francisco, Arturo, Dagoberto, Enrique, Jose, Ramiro, Adalberto, Aleida, Alejandro, Alfredo, Amalia.
Hispanic: probably an altered form of Spanish García (see Garcia ).

variants.name: Garcie
meanings: Altered form, under the French influence in LA, of Spanish García (see Garcia ). This form of the surname of Spanish origin is also found in southern France.
Spanish (Ávila): variant of García (see Garcia ).

variants.name: Garcilazo
meanings: Hispanic (mainly Mexico and Argentina): altered form of Spanish (Valencia) Garcilaso, a compound of the personal name Garci (a short form of García, see Garcia ) and the nickname laso ‘weak, exhausted’.

variants.name: Gacia
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish García (see Garcia ).

variants.name: Garsia
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of   Garcia  .

variants.name: Gracia
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Juan, Manuel, Francisco, Jesus, Luis, Guadalupe, Mario, Pedro, Javier, Jorge.

Spanish; Catalan (Gràcia): from a short form of the Christian religious epithet de la Gracia, meaning ‘of mercy’ and forming part of the Marian name Maria de la Gracia ‘(Holy) Mary of Grace’, from Spanish gracia, Catalan gràcia ‘grace, mercy’.
In some cases possibly also an altered form of Spanish García (see Garcia ).

variants.name: De Garcia
meanings: Spanish and Galician (De García); Portuguese: patronymic from Spanish and Galician García and Portuguese Garcia , with the preposition de ‘of’.

variants.name: Garces
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jorge, Carlos, Juan, Jose, Manuel, Cesar, Rafael, Luis, Fernando, Gustavo, Javier, Mario.  Portuguese Albeiro, Paulo.
Spanish; Catalan (Garcés); Portuguese (Garcês): patronymic from the personal name García (see Garcia ).

variants.name: Garzia
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Mario, Alfredo, Ernestina, Fernando, Juan, Pedro, Ricardo.  Italian Angelo, Antonio, Ciro, Corrado.
Italianized form of Spanish   Garcia  .

nameMeanings.name: DE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: HITA,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Kenji, Nobuo, Eriko, Hirohito, Hitoshi, Ichiro, Kanae, Kazuo, Makoto, Masato, Nobuyuki, Sadao.  Polish Andrzej, Czeslaw, Lukasz, Stanislaw, Zdzislaw.

Japanese: written 北 ‘north’, sometimes phonetically 喜田. It is found mostly in western Japan and Okinawa. Also sometimes pronounced Kida . In North America, some instances of the name may be the result of shortening other names beginning with Kita-.
Polish: nickname from kita ‘crest, bunch, sheaf; tail’.

variants.name: Kida
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Yoji, Akiyoshi, Eiko, Koji, Akiko, Haruo, Hiroyuki, Jitsuo, Keiichi, Koichi, Masatoshi, Reiko.
Japanese: written 木田 ‘rice paddy near a tree’. It is found in western Japan and the Ryūkyūan island of Yaeyama. Also sometimes pronounced   Kita  .

variants.name: Kitowski
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Czeslaw, Zigmond.
Polish: habitational name for someone from Kitów in Lublin Voivodeship.
Polish: derivative of the nickname   Kita  .

nameMeanings.name: señor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Andres, Blanca, Don Juan, Felipe, Valeria.
Spanish (Señor): nickname from the title señor‘mister’.

nameMeanings.name: Uceda,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Spanish: habitational name from a place called Uceda in Guadalajara, derived from Latin ulex ‘heather’, genitive ulicis, or from Latin ilex ‘holm oak’, genitive ilicis, with the colective suffix -edo. Compare Euceda .

variants.name: Euceda
meanings: Hispanic (mainly Honduras): altered form or perhaps an old variant of Spanish   Uceda  .

nameMeanings.name: Guadalajara
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Spanish: habitational name from Guadalajara in Castile, named with Arabic wādī-al-ḥijāra ‘river of the stones’.

nameMeanings.name: Fernando
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A modern form of the old Spanish name Ferdinando, which is of Germanic (Visigothic) origin, from farð ‘journey’ (or possibly an altered form of frið ‘peace’) + nand ‘ready, prepared’. The name was hereditary in the royal families of Spain from an early date. It was borne by Ferdinand I ( d. 1065 ) of Castile and Leon, who conducted successful campaigns against the Moors, and by his descendant Ferdinand V ( 1452–1516 ), who finally expelled the Moors from Spain altogether; he also gave financial backing to Columbus. Through the marriage in 1496 of his daughter Joan the Mad of Castile to the Habsburg Archduke Philip , the name Ferdinand also became hereditary in the Austrian imperial family. Their younger son, Ferdinand ( 1503–64 ), acquired the succession to the kingdoms of Hungary and Bohemia by marriage in 1521 , and became Holy Roman Emperor in 1558 . See Ferdinand .

variants.name: Fernán
meanings: A modern form of the old Spanish name Ferdinando, which is of Germanic (Visigothic) origin, from farð ‘journey’ (or possibly an altered form of frið ‘peace’) + nand ‘ready, prepared’. The name was hereditary in the royal families of Spain from an early date. It was borne by Ferdinand I ( d. 1065 ) of Castile and Leon, who conducted successful campaigns against the Moors, and by his descendant Ferdinand V ( 1452–1516 ), who finally expelled the Moors from Spain altogether; he also gave financial backing to Columbus. Through the marriage in 1496 of his daughter Joan the Mad of Castile to the Habsburg Archduke Philip , the name Ferdinand also became hereditary in the Austrian imperial family. Their younger son, Ferdinand ( 1503–64 ), acquired the succession to the kingdoms of Hungary and Bohemia by marriage in 1521 , and became Holy Roman Emperor in 1558 . See Ferdinand .

variants.name: Hernán
meanings: Variant of   Fernando .

variants.name: Hernando
meanings: Variant of   Fernando .

en.lifeSummary: When Fernando GARCÍA DE HITA, señor de Hita, Uceda, Guadalajara y Medinacelli was born in 1065, in Cillorigo de Liébana, Cantabria, Spain, his father, Conde Caballero Garcia Ordoñez de Aza Y de Nájera, was 15 and his mother, Urraca Garcés de Navarra, was 31. He married Trigidia Fernández de Aza in 1090, in Spain. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died about 1135, at the age of 71.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Fernando GARCÍA DE HITA, señor de Hita, Uceda, Guadalajara y Medinacelli est né en 1065, à Cillorigo de Liébana, Cantabria, Espagne, son père, Conde Caballero Garcia Ordoñez de Aza Y de Nájera, avait 15 ans et sa mère, Urraca Garcés de Navarra, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Trigidia Fernández de Aza en 1090, à Espagne. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé environ 1135, à l’âge de 71 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Fernando GARCÍA DE HITA, señor de Hita, Uceda, Guadalajara y Medinacelli 1065 in Cillorigo de Liébana, Cantabria, Spanien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Conde Caballero Garcia Ordoñez de Aza Y de Nájera 15 und seine Mutter Urraca Garcés de Navarra 31. Er hat 1090 in Spanien Trigidia Fernández de Aza geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb um 1135 im Alter von 71 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Fernando GARCÍA DE HITA, señor de Hita, Uceda, Guadalajara y Medinacelli è nato il nel 1065 nel Cillorigo de Liébana, Cantabria, Spagna, il suo padre, Conde Caballero Garcia Ordoñez de Aza Y de Nájera, aveva 15 anni e il suo madre, Urraca Garcés de Navarra, aveva 31 anni. Lui sposato Trigidia Fernández de Aza nel 1090, nel Spagna. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì circa 1135, all'età di 71 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Fernando GARCÍA DE HITA, señor de Hita, Uceda, Guadalajara y Medinacelli(이)가 1065년 스페인 캔타브리아 실로리고 데 리e바나에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Conde Caballero Garcia Ordoñez de Aza Y de Nájera(은)는 15세였고, 그의 어머니 Urraca Garcés de Navarra(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1090년 스페인에서 Trigidia Fernández de Aza(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1135년 71세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Fernando GARCÍA DE HITA, señor de Hita, Uceda, Guadalajara y Medinacelli nasceu em 1065, em Cillorigo de Liébana, Cantabria, Espanha, seu pai, Conde Caballero Garcia Ordoñez de Aza Y de Nájera, tinha 15 anos e sua mãe, Urraca Garcés de Navarra, tinha 31 anos. Ele casou-se com Trigidia Fernández de Aza em 1090, em Espanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1135, com 71 anos.

ru.Castrogeriz, Castrojeriz, Burgos, Castil: кастрогириз, кастроджириз, бургос, кастилла и леoн, Испания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Fernando GARCÍA DE HITA, señor de Hita, Uceda, Guadalajara y Medinacelli родился в 1065 г. в местности силлориго де лиeбана, кантабря, Испания, его отцу по имени Conde Caballero Garcia Ordoñez de Aza Y de Nájera было 15 лет, а матери, которую звали Urraca Garcés de Navarra, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Trigidia Fernández de Aza в 1090 г. в местности Испания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер около 1135 г. в возрасте 71 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Fernando GARCÍA DE HITA, señor de Hita, Uceda, Guadalajara y Medinacelli nació en 1065, en Cillorigo de Liébana, Cantabria, España, su papá, Conde Caballero Garcia Ordoñez de Aza Y de Nájera, tenía 15 años y su mamá, Urraca Garcés de Navarra, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Trigidia Fernández de Aza en 1090, en España. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Murió aproximadamente 1135, a la edad de 71 años.
